reasoning_content:
We need to determine if 22861 is a prime number. We'll check for divisibility by small primes.

First, check if it's even: 22861 is odd, so not divisible by 2.

Sum of digits: 2+2+8+6+1=19, not divisible by 3, so not divisible by 3.

Last digit: not 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5.

Check divisibility by 7: 7*3265=22855, remainder 6, so not divisible. Actually 7*3266=22862, too high, so 22861 mod 7 = 22861 - 7*3265 = 22861 - 22855 = 6, so no.

Check 11: alternating sum: (2-2+8-6+1)=3, not multiple of 11, so no.

13: 13*1758 = 22854, remainder 7; 13*1759=22867, too high, so no.

17: 17*1345 = 22865, too high; 17*1344=22848, remainder 13; so no.

19: 19*1203 = 22857, remainder 4; 19*1204=22876, too high; no.

23: 23*994 = 22862, too high; 23*993=22839, remainder 22; no.

29: 29*788 = 22852, remainder 9; 29*789=22881, too high; no.

31: 31*737 = 22847, remainder 14; 31*738=22878, too high; no.

37: 37*618 = 22866, too high; 37*617=22829, remainder 32; no.

41: 41*557 = 22837, remainder 24; 41*558=22878, too high; no.

43: 43*531 = 22833, remainder 28; 43*532=22876, too high; no.

47: 47*486 = 22842, remainder 19; 47*487=22889, too high; no.

53: 53*431 = 22843, remainder 18; 53*432=22896, too high; no.

59: 59*387 = 22833, remainder 28; 59*388=22892, too high; no.

61: 61*374 = 22814, remainder 47; 61*375=22875, too high; no.

67: 67*341 = 22847, remainder 14; 67*342=22914, too high; no.

71: 71*322 = 22862, too high; 71*321=22791, remainder 70; so no.

73: 73*313 = 22849, remainder 12; 73*314=22922, too high; no.

79: 79*289 = 22831, remainder 30; 79*290=22910, too high; no.

83: 83*275 = 22825, remainder 36; 83*276=22908, too high; no.

89: 89*256 = 22784, remainder 77; 89*257=22873, too high; no.

97: 97*235 = 22795, remainder 66; 97*236=22892, too high; no.

We need to check up to sqrt(22861). sqrt(22861) is about 151.2 (since 150^2=22500, 151^2=22801, 152^2=23104). So we need to check primes up to 151.

We already checked up to 97. Continue:

101: 101*226 = 22826, remainder 35; 101*227=22927, too high; no.

103: 103*222 = 22866, too high; 103*221=22763, remainder 98; no.

107: 107*213 = 22791, remainder 70; 107*214=22898, too high; no.

109: 109*209 = 22781, remainder 80; 109*210=22890, too high; no.

113: 113*202 = 22826, remainder 35; 113*203=22939, too high; no.

127: 127*180 = 22860, remainder 1; so 127*180=22860, leaving remainder 1, so not divisible. Actually 127*180=22860, so 22861-22860=1, so no.

131: 131*174 = 22794, remainder 67; 131*175=22925, too high; no.

137: 137*166 = 22742, remainder 119; 137*167=22879, too high; no.

139: 139*164 = 22796, remainder 65; 139*165=22935, too high; no.

149: 149*153 = 22797, remainder 64; 149*154=22946, too high; no.

151: 151*151 = 22801, so 151*151=22801, then 151*152=22952, too high. 22861-22801=60, not divisible.

We have checked all primes up to 151. None divided exactly. So 22861 is prime.

But wait, we missed some primes like 17? Already did. Also 19,23, etc. Also check 13? Did. Also check 7? Did. Also check 11? Did. Also check 31? Did. Also check 37? Did. Also check 41? Did. Also check 43? Did. Also check 47? Did. Also check 53? Did. Also check 59? Did. Also check 61? Did. Also check 67? Did. Also check 71? Did. Also check 73? Did. Also check 79? Did. Also check 83? Did. Also check 89? Did. Also check 97? Did. Also check 101,103,107,109,113,127,131,137,139,149,151. So no divisors.

Thus, 22861 is a prime number.

So answer: yes.
